title: Hadoop MapReduce Architecture: security Directory
description: The security directory in Hadoop MapReduce architecture is responsible for managing security-related functionality, such as encryption, authentication, and authorization. It includes classes such as IntermediateEncryptedStream, SecureShuffleUtils, SpillCallBackInjector, SpillCallBackPathsFinder, and TokenCache, which provide various security-related functionalities. The IntermediateEncryptedStream class is used for encrypting intermediate files during shuffle, while the SecureShuffleUtils class contains utility methods for generating and verifying hashes for shuffle. The SpillCallBackInjector class is used for injecting callbacks while spilling files, and the SpillCallBackPathsFinder class keeps track of spilled files and their positions. The TokenCache class provides user-facing APIs for transferring secrets from the job client to the tasks. These classes collaborate to provide secure and efficient security-related functionality in the Hadoop MapReduce framework.
